target.title: [codex] feat(agent): add session self-compaction action
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #6757.

OpenClaw can compact context automatically or through external/manual paths, but the in-run model had no first-class way to request compaction for its own active session when it detects context bloat. That makes self-directed context management awkward and pushes agents toward indirect workarounds.

## Why This Change Was Made

This PR adds a narrow first slice: `session_status({ action: "compact" })`.

- Extends the existing `session_status` schema with `action: "status" | "compact"` while preserving the current default status behavior.
- Uses a provider-portable flat enum schema for `action` (`{ type: "string", enum: [...] }`), not a TypeBox literal union / `anyOf` shape.
- Allows compaction requests only for the current/live session, not arbitrary visible sessions.
- Rejects combining `action: "compact"` with a model override, since those are different mutations.
- Routes the request through the embedded runner as an intentional abort and reuses the existing `preflightRecovery: { route: "compact_only", source: "mid-turn" }` path.
- Cleans synthetic aborted assistant artifacts before recovery so the transcript does not keep the aborted turn stub.
- Marks `session_status(action:"compact")` as mutating so replay/recovery logic does not treat it as a read-only status call.

This intentionally does not introduce a broad continuation framework or a separate compaction scheduler. Related broad work exists in #85651, but this PR is the small current-session self-compact action for #6757.

## User Impact

Agents can now explicitly ask OpenClaw to compact their own active session from inside a turn. The request fails closed for non-current sessions and unsupported execution contexts, and then resumes through the existing compaction retry machinery instead of performing transcript mutation inside the tool execution stack.

Existing `session_status` reads and model override behavior remain backward-compatible.

## Security / Maintainer Boundary

This PR deliberately limits the model-originated mutation boundary:

- The compact action is current-session only.
- Cross-session compaction through `session_status` is rejected even when a session is otherwise visible for status reads.
- The tool is available only when the embedded runner wires an active `onSelfCompact` callback; outside an active agent run it fails closed.
- The tool does not run compaction inline. It asks the runner to abort the current turn, clean abort artifacts, compact through the existing recovery path, then resume from transcript state.

Maintainer approval is still needed for exposing this current-session mutation as a model-callable tool action.

## Evidence

Targeted behavior tests:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts src/agents/tool-mutation.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.cwd-split.test.ts

[test] passed 2 Vitest shards
```

Embedded compaction/recovery regression tests:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run.overflow-compaction.loop.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard
```

Static checks:

```text
node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.test.src.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/test-src.tsbuildinfo
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts
node_modules/.bin/oxlint --config .oxlintrc.json src/agents/tools/session-status-tool.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts
git diff --check
```

Live active-run proof, redacted local run:

I ran a real `runEmbeddedAgent` path against a local mock OpenAI Responses server with only `session_status` allowed. The mock model first emitted `session_status({ "action": "compact", "sessionKey": "current" })`; OpenClaw then aborted the active turn, compacted, and resumed into a second model call.

Observed runner log excerpt:

```text
[agent/embedded] context overflow detected (attempt 1/3); attempting auto-compaction for openai/gpt-5.5
[agent/embedded] auto-compaction succeeded for openai/gpt-5.5; retrying prompt
[agents/post-compaction-guard] post-compaction guard armed for 3 attempts
```

Redacted proof summary:

```json
{
  "requestCount": 2,
  "firstModelCall": {
    "toolNamesOffered": ["session_status"],
    "actionSchema": { "type": "string", "enum": ["status", "compact"] }
  },
  "compactRequest": {
    "toolResultCompactRequested": true,
    "toolResultText": "Self-compaction requested. The current turn will stop, compact context, and resume from the transcript."
  },
  "abortAndCleanup": {
    "hasRawAbortReasonInTranscript": false,
    "toolResultEntries": 1
  },
  "compaction": {
    "transcriptCompactionEntries": 1,
    "metaCompactionCount": 1,
    "metaCompactionTokensAfter": 146
  },
  "resume": {
    "resumeModelCallObserved": true,
    "resumeInputIncludesCompactionEntry": true,
    "finalMarkerSeen": true,
    "finalAssistantVisibleText": "OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_SELF_COMPACT_RESUMED"
  },
  "transcript": {
    "assistantEntries": 2,
    "toolUseAssistantEntries": 1,
    "finalAssistantEntries": 1
  }
}
```

The final transcript tail contained the tool-call assistant message, the `session_status` tool result, one `compaction` entry, and the resumed final assistant message with `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_SELF_COMPACT_RESUMED`; it did not contain the raw self-compaction abort reason.

Notes:

- `pnpm tsgo:test:src` initially triggered `pnpm install` and stalled on optional platform package downloads from npm, so I stopped it and ran the underlying `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs ...` command directly. The direct typecheck passed.
- Exact `"#6757"` open PR search returned no active direct PR before this branch was pushed.
